headline: Senior Citizen Discovers TikTok At Age 87, Immediately Becomes 'Grandpa Danger,' Gains 47 Million Followers
summary: Harold 'Grandpa Danger' posts videos of himself doing dangerous stunts, confusing young viewers about aging.
fullBody: Harold Oldman, 87, has become the internet's newest sensation by posting TikTok videos of himself attempting increasingly dangerous stunts. His account, 'Grandpa Danger,' features videos like 'Jumping on the bed at my age: 87th attempt' and 'Eating a whole cake: my cardiologist's nightmare.' The account has gained 47 million followers in 3 months, with young viewers saying things like 'This man is 87 and I'm afraid to go to the gym' and 'I've never felt so inadequate in my life.' Harold says he started the account because 'I'm bored, and my grandkids said the youth love danger.' His doctors have issued statements saying 'Please stop, we don't want to explain to the media how our patient died filming a TikTok.' Harold has responded by filming his doctors' reactions.
imagePrompt: 87-year-old man doing skateboard trick with gray hair flying, young people watching in shock, TikTok logo, comic illustration, flat design, purple and yellow colors
